g.data-narration: Clustering is an unsupervised learning technique that groups similar data points without predefined labels.
g.text: Task of dividing data points into groups (clusters) such that:
Points in same cluster are MORE similar
Points in different clusters are DISSIMILAR

g.data-narration: Unlike supervised learning with labeled data, clustering lets the data speak by finding patterns automatically.
Clustering approaches are categorized by their mechanics: centroid-based, density-based, hierarchical, and distribution-based.
K-Means is a centroid-based algorithm that partitions data into K clusters by minimizing within-cluster variance.
g.text: One of the simplest and most widely used algorithms. Partitions data into K clusters.
Core idea: Minimize within-cluster sum of squares (WCSS)

g.data-narration: Step 1: Choose K (number of clusters). Step 2: Initialize K centroids randomly from data points.
Assignment Step: Each data point is assigned to the nearest centroid based on Euclidean distance.
g.text: Assign each data point to the CLOSEST centroid using distance metric.

g.data-narration: Update Step: Recalculate centroids as the mean of all points in each cluster, then repeat until convergence.
g.text: Recalculate centroids as the MEAN of all assigned points.
μⱼ = (1/|Cⱼ|) Σ xᵢ for all xᵢ in cluster Cⱼ
Repeat Assignment → Update until centroids stop changing (convergence)

g.data-narration: K-Means has challenges: K must be predefined, it's sensitive to initialization, and struggles with non-spherical clusters.
DBSCAN is density-based clustering that finds arbitrarily shaped clusters and identifies outliers without needing K.
g.text: Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise
• Can find clusters of arbitrary shape

g.data-narration: DBSCAN classifies points as Core (dense region), Border (reachable from core), or Noise (outliers).
DBSCAN finds core points, expands clusters by adding density-reachable points, and marks unassigned points as noise.
g.text: 1. For each point, count points within ε (epsilon) radius
2. If Core Point → form new cluster, add all density-reachable points
3. Repeat for unvisited core points. Remaining = Noise

g.data-narration: K-Means requires K and works best for spherical clusters. DBSCAN finds arbitrary shapes and handles outliers automatically.
g.text: Requires K • Spherical clusters • Sensitive to outliers
No K needed • Any shape • Handles noise automatically

g.data-narration: Clustering is fundamental for exploratory data analysis. Choose algorithm based on data shape, size, and noise handling needs.
g.text: Clustering groups unlabeled data by similarity • Intra-cluster high, Inter-cluster low
No universal best algorithm - choose based on data distribution, cluster shape, and noise handling
